title: HIT-CGVR OpenGL Labs
summary: Four progressive OpenGL 3.3 computer-graphics labs from Harbin Institute of Technology's CG & VR course (2024 Spring). Each lab is an independent C++/GLFW/GLAD project; they form a pedagogical sequence where each adds one rendering feature on top of the previous. All core subsystems are left as TODO stubs to be implemented.
instructionPreview: This task contains four OpenGL 3.3 computer-graphics labs (lab1–lab4) from an undergraduate computer-graphics and VR sequence. Each lab is a C++ / GLFW / GLAD project with its own `CMakeLists.txt` in `/workspace/lab<N>/`.
instruction:
This task contains four OpenGL 3.3 computer-graphics labs (lab1–lab4) from an
undergraduate computer-graphics and VR sequence. Each lab is a C++ / GLFW / GLAD project with its own
`CMakeLists.txt` in `/workspace/lab<N>/`.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present and each
lab will compile with `cmake -S lab<N> -B lab<N>/build && cmake --build lab<N>/build`.

All core subsystems have been left as `/* TODO: implement */` stubs. You need to
implement all the modules and features listed below so that all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

- **Shader system** (`lab1/src/shader.cpp`, `lab2/src/shader.cpp`, `lab3/src/shader.cpp`,
  `lab4/src/shader.cpp`): Each lab has a `Shader` class that loads, compiles, and links
  GLSL vertex and fragment shaders into an OpenGL program. Labs 2–4 also require uniform
  setter methods for bool, int, float, mat4, vec3, and vec2 types. The class stores the
  compiled program handle in the `ID` member. (~4 files, ~9 functions each)

- **Camera system** (`lab2/src/camera.h`, `lab3/src/camera.h`, `lab4/src/camera.h`):
  An Euler-angle FPS camera that computes a view matrix, processes keyboard/mouse/scroll
  input, and maintains internal direction vectors. The camera tracks Position, Front, Up,
  Right vectors and Yaw/Pitch angles. (~3 files, ~5 methods each)

- **Lab 1 — Triangle rendering** (`lab1/src/main.cpp`, `lab1/res/expr1.vs`,
  `lab1/res/expr1.fs`): The entire `main()` function must be implemented: GLFW/GLAD
  initialization, window creation, triangle vertex data definition, VAO/VBO setup,
  shader loading, render loop, and cleanup. The vertex shader passes through positions;
  the fragment shader outputs a constant colour. (~3 files)

- **Lab 2 — Textured diamond** (`lab2/src/main.cpp`): VAO/VBO/EBO setup for the diamond
  geometry, indexed draw call, and a `loadTexture()` function that loads images with
  stb_image, uploads to GPU, generates mipmaps, and configures texture parameters.
  (~1 file, ~2 regions)

- **Lab 3 — Phong-lit cube** (`lab3/src/main.cpp`, `lab3/res/shader/phong.vert`,
  `lab3/res/shader/phong.frag`): Cube VAO/VBO setup with positions, tex coords, and
  normals. The vertex shader transforms normals to world space and computes fragment
  position. The fragment shader implements the Phong lighting model (ambient, diffuse,
  specular). Uniforms for light and camera must be sent each frame. (~3 files)

- **Lab 4 — Off-screen FBO + circle** (`lab4/src/main.cpp`,
  `lab4/res/shader/circle.frag`): Full-screen quad VAO/VBO setup, framebuffer object
  creation with colour texture and depth-stencil renderbuffer attachments, two-pass
  rendering (off-screen circle pass followed by on-screen display pass), and a circle
  fragment shader with aspect-ratio correction. (~2 files)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions/methods are called directly by the test suite. You must
implement them with these exact signatures:

- `Shader::Shader(const char *vertexPath, const char *fragmentPath)` — reads, compiles,
  and links vertex/fragment shaders into an OpenGL program stored in `ID`
- `void Shader::use()` — activates the shader program
- `void Shader::setBool(const std::string& name, bool value) const` — sets a bool uniform
- `void Shader::setInt(const std::string& name, int value) const` — sets an int uniform
- `void Shader::setFloat(const std::string& name, float value) const` — sets a float uniform
- `void Shader::setMat4(const std::string& name, glm::mat4 value) const` — sets a mat4 uniform
- `void Shader::setVec3(const std::string& name, glm::vec3 value) const` — sets a vec3 uniform
- `void Shader::setVec3(const std::string& name, float x, float y, float z) const` — sets a vec3 from components
- `void Shader::setVec2(const std::string& name, float x, float y) const` — sets a vec2 uniform
- `glm::mat4 Camera::GetViewMatrix()` — returns the view matrix using lookAt
- `void Camera::ProcessKeyboard(Camera_Movement direction, float deltaTime)` — translates camera position
- `void Camera::ProcessMouseMovement(float xoffset, float yoffset, GLboolean constrainPitch)` — rotates camera
- `void Camera::ProcessMouseScroll(float yoffset)` — zooms camera via FOV
- `unsigned int loadTexture(const char *path)` — loads an image and returns an OpenGL texture ID

moduleDag.description: Four progressive OpenGL 3.3 computer-graphics labs from Harbin Institute of Technology's CG & VR course (2024 Spring). Each lab is an independent C++/GLFW/GLAD project; they form a pedagogical sequence where each adds one rendering feature on top of the previous. All core subsystems are left as TODO stubs to be implemented.

nodes.label: Lab 1 — Shader System & GLSL Shaders
nodes.description: The basic Shader class: reads vertex and fragment GLSL source from disk, compiles both shader objects, links them into a program, stores the handle in the ID member, and cleans up individual shader objects. The GLSL pair implements a simple position passthrough (vertex) and constant blue-tint colour output (fragment).

nodes.label: Lab 1 — Triangle Main
nodes.description: Initialises GLFW and configures an OpenGL 3.3 core-profile context, loads function pointers via GLAD, creates a window, defines three NDC-space triangle vertices, sets up a VAO/VBO with one vertex attribute, instantiates the Shader, and runs the render loop (clear, draw, swap buffers). Releases GPU resources on exit.

nodes.label: Lab 2 — Full Shader Uniforms & FPS Camera
nodes.description: Extends the Lab1 shader compile/link pattern with typed uniform setter methods (setBool, setInt, setFloat, setMat4, setVec3 × 2, setVec2). Adds the Euler-angle FPS Camera class with GetViewMatrix (glm::lookAt), ProcessKeyboard (WASD translation), ProcessMouseMovement (yaw/pitch update with 89° clamp), ProcessMouseScroll (FOV zoom), and the internal updateCameraVectors helper.

nodes.label: Lab 2 — Textured Diamond Main
nodes.description: Diamond geometry setup (VAO/VBO/EBO with interleaved position and UV attributes, 24 indices). Implements loadTexture() which uses stb_image to load images, selects GL_RED/RGB/RGBA format from channel count, uploads to GPU, generates mipmaps, and sets wrap/filter parameters. The render loop draws indexed geometry each frame with view/projection matrices from the camera.

nodes.label: Lab 3 — Shader, Camera & Phong GLSL
nodes.description: Replicates the full-uniform shader and FPS camera from Lab2 within Lab3's codebase. Adds the Phong GLSL pair: the vertex shader transforms normals to world space (via the normal matrix) and emits world-space fragment position; the fragment shader computes ambient, diffuse, and specular contributions (256-shininess specular) multiplied by the sampled texture colour.

nodes.label: Lab 3 — Phong-Lit Cube Main
nodes.description: Cube geometry setup (VAO/VBO with 36 vertices in interleaved position/UV/normal format, 8-float stride, three vertex attributes). The render loop sends per-frame uniforms for model, view, projection, lightColor, lightPos, and camera Position to the Phong shader before each draw call.

nodes.label: Lab 4 — Shader, Camera & Circle Fragment Shader
nodes.description: Replicates the full-uniform shader and FPS camera from Lab3 within Lab4's codebase. Adds the circle fragment shader that remaps UV coordinates to a centred [-1, 1] space, applies aspect-ratio correction via the w_div_h uniform, computes the distance from the centre, and outputs a binary circle-border mask.

nodes.label: Lab 4 — Off-Screen FBO & Two-Pass Rendering Main
nodes.description: Full-screen quad VAO/VBO setup (position + UV, stride 5 floats, two attributes). Framebuffer object creation with a GL_RGB colour texture attachment and a GL_DEPTH24_STENCIL8 renderbuffer; completeness checked before unbinding. Two-pass render loop: Pass 1 binds the FBO and runs the circle shader (with w_div_h uniform); Pass 2 binds the default framebuffer, disables depth test, and renders the FBO texture to the screen quad.

edges.label: lab1/src/main.cpp #includes shader.h and instantiates the Shader class — it will not compile until shader.cpp (and the GLSL files it loads at runtime) are implemented

lab2 is the next course lab; its Shader extends lab1's compile/link pattern with uniform setters, and the Camera class is the new subsystem introduced in lab2 — both follow the same OpenGL idioms established in lab1
lab2/src/main.cpp calls shader.setInt() for the texture unit and camera.GetViewMatrix()/ProcessKeyboard() — it will not compile without the shader uniform setters and camera methods being present
lab3 is the next course lab; its shader and camera are direct copies of lab2's completed implementations, and the Phong GLSL shaders build on the texture-and-camera pipeline established in lab2

lab3/src/main.cpp calls the full suite of shader uniform setters (setMat4, setVec3) to upload Phong lighting data each frame, and the Phong GLSL shaders must be complete before the cube renders correctly

lab4 is the next course lab; its shader and camera replicate lab3's completed versions, and the circle fragment shader is a new effect introduced in lab4 that extends the rendering pipeline mastered in lab3

lab4/src/main.cpp uses the Shader's setFloat() to send the w_div_h aspect-ratio uniform, and the circle fragment shader must be in place for the off-screen pass — neither the FBO setup nor the two-pass loop can be tested without them
